@graph.name: Land Rover models fitted with the Ingenium 2.0 Petrol (204PT)

itemListElement.name: Land Rover Discovery Sport L550 P200/P250

mainEntity.name: How much does an Ingenium 2.0 petrol engine replacement cost?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned Ingenium 2.0 petrol typically costs £3,000–£5,000 supply only, varying by power output and specific sub-variant. Fitting adds £900–£1,700 depending on labour rates and vehicle model.

mainEntity.name: Is the Ingenium 2.0 petrol a reliable engine?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes — the 204PT is more reliable than its diesel counterpart (4.0★). Its direct-injection turbo design is well-proven, though cooling system components require regular maintenance and the water pump is a known wear item.

mainEntity.name: What is the most common Ingenium 2.0 petrol failure?

acceptedAnswer.text: Water pump failure and coolant leaks from plastic fittings are the most common issues — typically occurring from 60,000–80,000 miles. Other reported problems include occasional timing chain stretch and PCV valve failure.

mainEntity.name: Which Land Rover models use the Ingenium 2.0 petrol?

acceptedAnswer.text: It powers the Discovery Sport (L550), Evoque (L551), Velar (L560), and Defender (L663) — spanning 200–300 hp outputs across P200, P250, and P300 variants.

mainEntity.name: Is the Land Rover Ingenium 2.0 petrol the same as Jaguar's version?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes — the 204PT engine code is shared across both brands. The engine is mechanically identical between Land Rover and Jaguar fitments, though ECU calibrations and ancillaries differ by model.

costGuide.labourEstimate: Fitting an Ingenium 2.0 petrol typically takes 10–14 hours at £65–£95/hr (independent) or £110–£160/hr (London/SE dealer) [EM-QUOTE].
costGuide.sharedCostNote: Parts cost is identical across Land Rover and Jaguar applications; labour time varies slightly by engine bay access and model‑specific subframe removal.
